name: Testcontainers for Node.js

auth.notes: Docker daemon required; no API auth

performance.notes: Container startup overhead (5-30s depending on image); cached after first pull; fast for subsequent runs

agent_readiness.notes: Docker-based integration testing for Node.js. Spawn real PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, Elasticsearch, LocalStack containers in tests. Pre-built modules: PostgreSqlContainer, MySqlContainer, RedisContainer, KafkaContainer, etc. Automatic cleanup after tests. Works with Jest, Vitest, Mocha. Ryuk resource reaper for container cleanup. Reuse containers across test suites for speed. TypeScript types. Mirror of Java testcontainers.

gotchas: Requires Docker Desktop or Docker daemon running
Container startup adds significant test duration (first run)
Resource intensive — many containers slow CI pipelines
Port binding conflicts if tests run in parallel without isolation
best_when: Database migration testing against real DB engine
Testing message queue interactions with real Kafka/RabbitMQ
LocalStack for AWS service integration tests
avoid_when: Teams needing fast test suites (startup time is significant)
summary: Testcontainers for Node.js spawns real Docker containers (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, MySQL, MongoDB, LocalStack) in integration tests. Pre-built modules with automatic cleanup via Ryuk. TypeScript types. Works with Jest and Vitest. Container reuse mode for faster subsequent runs. Requires Docker. Essential for Node.js integration tests needing real infrastructure without mocking.
